text: Lesson 52
zh: 第52课

text: Mud is mud
zh: 泥巴就是泥巴

text: Why did Harry decide to give up his little game?
zh: 哈里为什么决定放弃他那个小把戏？

text: My cousin, Harry, keeps a large curiously-shaped bottle on permanent display in his study.
zh: 我表哥哈里在他的书房里常年摆着一个形状奇特的大瓶子。

text: Despite the fact that the bottle is tinted a delicate shade of green,
zh: 尽管这个瓶子被染成了淡淡的绿色，

text: an observant visitor would soon notice that it is filled with what looks like a thick, greyish substance.
zh: 细心的访客很快就会发现，瓶子里装的东西看起来像是一种灰乎乎的浓稠物质。

text: If you were to ask Harry what was in the bottle, he would tell you that it contained perfumed mud.
zh: 如果你问哈里瓶子里装的是什么，他会告诉你那是香水泥。

text: If you expressed doubt or surprise, he would immediately invite you to smell it and then to rub some into your skin.
zh: 如果你表示怀疑或惊讶，他会立刻请你闻一闻，再把一些抹到皮肤上试试。

text: The brief experiment would dispel any further doubts she might have.
zh: 这个简短的实验会打消她可能还残留的任何疑虑。

text: The bottle really does contain perfumed mud.
zh: 瓶子里装的确实是香水泥。

text: How Harry came into the possession of this outlandish stuff makes an interesting story which he is fond of relating.
zh: 哈里是如何弄到这种稀奇古怪的东西的，说来是个有趣的故事，他也很爱讲。

text: Furthermore, the acquisition of this bottle cured him of a bad habit he had been developing for years.
zh: 而且，买下这个瓶子还治好了他多年来养成的一个坏习惯。

text: Harry used to consider it a great joke to go into expensive cosmetic shops and make outrageous requests for goods that do not exist.
zh: 哈里过去总觉得走进高档化妆品店，荒唐地要求购买根本不存在的商品，是件很好玩的事。

text: He would invent fanciful names on the spot.
zh: 他会当场编造出一些异想天开的名字。

text: On entering a shop,
zh: 一走进店里，

text: he would ask for a new perfume called 'Scented Shadow' or for 'insoluble bath cubes'.
zh: 他就会问有没有一种叫“香氛暗影”的新香水，或者“不溶性沐浴块”。

text: If a shop assistant told him she had not heard of it, he would pretend to be considerably put out.
zh: 如果店员告诉他没听说过这种东西，他就会假装大为不满。

text: He loved to be told that one of his imaginary products was temporarily out of stock
zh: 他最喜欢听到店员说他编造的某样产品暂时缺货，

text: and he would faithfully promise to call again at some future date, but of course he never did.
zh: 然后他会信誓旦旦地保证改天再来，但当然他从未再去过。

text: How Harry managed to keep a straight face during these performances is quite beyond me.
zh: 哈里在这些表演中是如何保持面不改色的，我实在想不通。

text: Harry does not need to be prompted to explain how he bought his precious bottle of mud.
zh: 不用别人催促，哈里就会主动讲起他是怎么买到那瓶珍贵泥巴的。

text: One day, he went to an exclusive shop in London and asked for 'Myrolite'.
zh: 有一天，他去伦敦一家高档商店，要买“Myrolite”。

text: The shop assistant looked puzzled and Harry repeated the word, slowly stressing each syllable.
zh: 店员一脸茫然，哈里又重复了一遍这个词，慢慢地把每个音节都咬得很清楚。

text: When the woman shook her head in bewilderment,
zh: 那位女店员困惑地摇了摇头，

text: Harry went on to explain that 'myrolite' was a hard, amber-like substance which could be used to remove freckles.
zh: 哈里接着解释说，“myrolite”是一种坚硬的、琥珀般的物质，可以用来去除雀斑。

text: This explanation evidently conveyed something to the woman who searched shelf after shelf.
zh: 这番解释显然让那位女店员想到了什么，她把一排排货架都找了个遍。

text: She produced all sorts of weird concoctions, but none of them met with Harry's requirements.
zh: 她拿出了各种稀奇古怪的调制品，但没有一样符合哈里的要求。

text: When Harry put on his act of being mildly annoyed, the assistant promised to order some for him.
zh: 当哈里装出略显不悦的样子时，店员答应替他订购一些。

text: Intoxicated by his success, Harry then asked for perfumed mud.
zh: 被这次成功冲昏了头脑，哈里接着又提出要买香水泥。

text: He expected the assistant to look at him in blank astonishment.
zh: 他本以为店员会一脸茫然地看着他。

text: However, it was his turn to be surprised, for the woman's eyes immediately lit up
zh: 然而，这次轮到他吃惊了，因为那位女店员的眼睛顿时一亮，

text: and she fetched several bottles which she placed on the counter for Harry to inspect.
zh: 她取来了好几瓶，放在柜台上让哈里挑选。

text: For once, Harry had to admit defeat.
zh: 这一次，哈里不得不承认自己输了。

text: He picked up what seemed to be the smallest bottle and discreetly asked the price.
zh: 他拿起看起来最小的那一瓶，小心翼翼地问了价钱。

text: He was glad to get away with a mere twenty pounds and he beat a hasty retreat, clutching the precious bottle under his arm.
zh: 他庆幸只花了二十英镑就脱身，赶紧夹着那瓶珍贵的东西匆匆离去。

text: From then on, Harry decided that this little game he had invented might prove to be expensive.
zh: 从那以后，哈里意识到，他自己发明的这个小把戏可能代价不菲。

text: The curious bottle which now adorns the bookcase in his study was his first and last purchase of rare cosmetics.
zh: 如今摆在他书房书柜上的那只奇特瓶子，是他第一次、也是最后一次购买稀罕化妆品。
